hints: Order matters: check for negative first (continue to skip), then check for > 100 (break to stop), then append. Put the checks before result.append(n).
continue jumps back to the top of the loop for the next value — the append() line below it is never reached for that iteration.
break exits the loop entirely — not just for that one value, but for all remaining values too. The value that triggered break is not appended.
If your result includes the value > 100, you're appending before breaking. Move the break check to before result.append(n).
